records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.52 - It has been narrated: "There is no harm if the left hand is incapacitated (or missing)."
records.arabicText: 52 - وَ قَدْ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّهُ لاَ بَأْسَ إِذَا كَانَ اَلْيَسَارُ مُعْتَلَّةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.52 - It has been narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if the left hand is incapacitated (or missing)."

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.53 - Hisham ibn Salim asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) saying:
"Is it permissible for me to perform ghusl (ritual washing) from major impurity (junub) and other reasons in a restroom where people urinate, while wearing Sindhi sandals?"
Imam (as) said: "If the water flowing from your body reaches the soles of your feet, then do not wash the soles of your feet”.
records.arabicText: 53 - وَ سَأَلَ هِشَامُ بْنُ سَالِمٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَقَالَ لَهُ: أَغْتَسِلُ مِنَ اَلْجَنَابَةِ وَ غَيْرِ ذَلِكَ فِي اَلْكَنِيفِ اَلَّذِي يُبَالُ فِيهِ وَ عَلَيَّ نَعْلٌ سِنْدِيَّةٌ فَأَغْتَسِلُ وَ عَلَيَّ اَلنَّعْلُ كَمَا هِيَ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ اَلْمَاءُ اَلَّذِي يَسِيلُ مِنْ جَسَدِكَ يُصِيبُ أَسْفَلَ قَدَمَيْكَ فَلاَ تَغْسِلْ أَسْفَلَ قَدَمَيْكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.53 - Hisham ibn Salim asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) saying:
"Is it permissible for me to perform ghusl (ritual washing) from major impurity (junub) and other reasons in a restroom where people urinate, while wearing Sindhi sandals?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If the water flowing from your body reaches the soles of your feet, then do not wash the soles of your feet”.

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.54 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about how a man should sit when he wants to perform istinja (cleaning oneself after using the toilet).
Imam (as) said: "As he would sit for relieving himself."
records.arabicText: 54 - وَ سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَسْتَنْجِيَ كَيْفَ يَقْعُدُ قَالَ «كَمَا يَقْعُدُ لِلْغَائِطِ.

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.55 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"When a man urinates, he should not touch his private parts with his right hand."
records.arabicText:
`
55 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا بَالَ اَلرَّجُلُ فَلاَ يَمَسَّ ذَكَرَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.55 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a man urinates, he should not touch his private parts with his right hand."

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.56 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Prolonged sitting in the restroom causes hemorrhoids”.
records.arabicText: 56 - وَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «طُولُ اَلْجُلُوسِ عَلَى اَلْخَلاَءِ يُورِثُ اَلْبَاسُورَ.

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.57 - Omar ibn Yazid asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the recitation of supplications in the restroom and reading the Qur'an.
Imam (as) said: "It is not permitted to recite more than the Ayat al-Kursi or to praise Allah (swt) (by saying) 'All praise is due to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the worlds' while in the restroom."
records.arabicText: 57 - وَ سَأَلَ عُمَرُ بْنُ يَزِيدَ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلتَّسْبِيحِ فِي اَلْمَخْرَجِ وَ قِرَاءَةِ اَلْقُرْآنِ فَقَالَ «لَمْ يُرَخَّصْ فِي اَلْكَنِيفِ أَكْثَرَ مِنْ آيَةِ اَلْكُرْسِيِّ وَ يَحْمَدَ اَللَّهَ أَوْ آيَةِ «اَلْحَمْدُ لِلّٰهِ رَبِّ اَلْعٰالَمِينَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.57 - Omar ibn Yazid asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the recitation of supplications in the restroom and reading the Qur'an.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is not permitted to recite more than the Ayat al-Kursi or to praise Allah (swt) (by saying) 'All praise is due to Allah (swt), the Lord (azj) of the worlds' while in the restroom."

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.58 - When Allah (swt) spoke to Musa ibn Imran (as),
Musa (as) asked: "O’ Lord (azj), are You far from me that I should call to You, or are You near that I may converse with You?"
Allah (swt), Glorious and Exalted, revealed to him: "I am with the one who remembers Me. "
Musa (as) then said: "O’ Lord (azj), there are circumstances in which I hold You in such reverence that I would not mention You."
Allah (swt) said: "O’ Musa, remember Me in all circumstances."
records.arabicText: 58 - وَ لَمَّا نَاجَى اَللَّهُ مُوسَى بْنَ عِمْرَانَ [عَلَى نَبِيِّنَا وَ] عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ مُوسَى: يَا رَبِّ أَ بَعِيدٌ أَنْتَ مِنِّي فَأُنَادِيَكَ أَمْ قَرِيبٌ فَأُنَاجِيَكَ فَأَوْحَى اَللَّهُ جَلَّ جَلاَلُهُ إِلَيْهِ «أَنَا جَلِيسُ مَنْ ذَكَرَنِي » فَقَالَ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَا رَبِّ إِنِّي أَكُونُ فِي أَحْوَالٍ أُجِلُّكَ أَنْ أَذْكُرَكَ فِيهَا فَقَالَ «يَا مُوسَى اُذْكُرْنِي عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ».

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.59 - People used to perform istinja (cleaning after relieving oneself) with stones, and a man from the Ansar ate some food, which upset his stomach. He then performed istinja with water. Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, revealed:
"Indeed, Allah (swt) loves those who repent and loves those who purify themselves." (Surah Al Baqarah 2:222).
The Messenger of Allah (sw) called him, and the man feared that something negative had been revealed about him. When he entered, the Prophet asked him, "Have you done anything today?"
He replied: "Yes, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw), I ate some food, which upset my stomach, so I performed istinja with water."
The Prophet then said: "Rejoice! For Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, has revealed concerning you: 'Indeed, Allah (swt) loves those who repent and loves those who purify themselves.' You are among the first to repent and among the first to purify themselves."
records.arabicText:
59 - وَ كَانَ اَلنَّاسُ يَسْتَنْجُونَ بِالْأَحْجَارِ فَأَكَلَ رَجُلٌ مِنَ اَلْأَنْصَارِ طَعَاماً فَلاَنَ بَطْنُهُ فَاسْتَنْجَى بِالْمَاءِ فَأَنْزَلَ اَللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى فِيهِ: «۝ إِنَّ اَللّٰهَ يُحِبُّ اَلتَّوّٰابِينَ وَ يُحِبُّ اَلْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ ۝»
فَدَعَاهُ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ فَخَشِيَ اَلرَّجُلُ أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدْ نَزَلَ فِيهِ أَمْرٌ يَسُوؤُهُ فَلَمَّا دَخَلَ قَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ «هَلْ عَمِلْتَ فِي يَوْمِكَ هَذَا شَيْئاً» قَالَ نَعَمْ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ أَكَلْتُ طَعَاماً فَلاَنَ بَطْنِي فَاسْتَنْجَيْتُ بِالْمَاءِ فَقَالَ لَهُ «أَبْشِرْ فَإِنَّ اَللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى قَدْ أَنْزَلَ فِيكَ: «إِنَّ اَللّٰهَ يُحِبُّ اَلتَّوّٰابِينَ وَ يُحِبُّ اَلْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ» فَكُنْتَ أَنْتَ أَوَّلَ اَلتَّوَّابِينَ وَ أَوَّلَ اَلْمُتَطَهِّرِينَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.59 - People used to perform istinja (cleaning after relieving oneself) with stones, and a man from the Ansar ate some food, which upset his stomach. He then performed istinja with water. Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, revealed:
"Indeed, Allah (swt) loves those who repent and loves those who purify themselves." (Surah Al Baqarah 2:222).
The Messenger of Allah (sw) called him, and the man feared that something negative had been revealed about him. When he entered, the Prophet asked him,
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Have you done anything today?"
He replied: "Yes, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw), I ate some food, which upset my stomach, so I performed istinja with water."
The Prophet then said: "Rejoice! For Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, has revealed concerning you: 'Indeed, Allah (swt) loves those who repent and loves those who purify themselves.' You are among the first to repent and among the first to purify themselves."

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.60 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
"In istinja, what appears on the anal area should be washed, but the fingers should not be inserted into it."
records.arabicText: 60 - وَقَالَ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «فِي اَلاِسْتِنْجَاءِ يُغْسَلُ مَا ظَهَرَ عَلَى اَلشَّرْجِ وَ لاَ يُدْخَلُ فِيهِ اَلْأَنْمُلَةُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.60 - Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "In istinja, what appears on the anal area should be washed, but the fingers should not be inserted into it."

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.61 - It has been narrated:
"Whoever speaks while in the restroom will not have his needs fulfilled”.
records.arabicText: 61 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ مَنْ تَكَلَّمَ عَلَى اَلْخَلاَءِ لَمْ تُقْضَ حَاجَتُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.61 - It has been narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever speaks while in the restroom will not have his needs fulfilled”.

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.62 - The Prophet, said to some of his wives:
"Tell the believing women to perform istinja with water and to be thorough in it, for it is a means of purification for the private parts and helps prevent hemorrhoids."
records.arabicText: 62 - وَ إِنَّ اَلنَّبِيَّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ لِبَعْضِ نِسَائِهِ: «مُرِي اَلنِّسَاءَ اَلْمُؤْمِنَاتِ أَنْ يَسْتَنْجِينَ بِالْمَاءِ وَ يُبَالِغْنَ فَإِنَّهُ مَطْهَرَةٌ لِلْحَوَاشِي وَ مَذْهَبَةٌ لِلْبَوَاسِيرِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.62 - The Prophet, said to some of his wives:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Tell the believing women to perform istinja with water and to be thorough in it, for it is a means of purification for the private parts and helps prevent hemorrhoids."

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.63 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, has appointed angels to protect the vegetation of the earth, including trees and palm trees. There is no tree or palm except that there is an angel with it from Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, who guards it. If it were not for the presence of those angels to protect them, wild animals and pests would consume their fruits."
records.arabicText: 63 - مَا قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ لِلَّهِ تَبَارَكَ وتَعَالَى مَلاَئِكَةً وَكَّلَهُمْ بِنَبَاتِ اَلْأَرْضِ مِنَ اَلشَّجَرِ وَ اَلنَّخْلِ فَلَيْسَ مِنْ شَجَرَةٍ وَ لاَ نَخْلَةٍ إِلاَّ وَ مَعَهَا مِنَ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مَلَكٌ يَحْفَظُهَا وَ مَا كَانَ مِنْهَا وَ لَوْ لاَ أَنَّ مَعَهَا مَنْ يَمْنَعُهَا لَأَكَلَتْهَا اَلسِّبَاعُ وَ هَوَامُّ اَلْأَرْضِ إِذَا كَانَ فِيهَا ثَمَرَتُهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.63 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, Allah (swt), Blessed and Exalted, has appointed angels to protect the vegetation of the earth, including trees and palm trees. There is no tree or palm except that there is an angel with it from Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, who guards it. If it were not for the presence of those angels to protect them, wild animals and pests would consume their fruits."

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.64 – (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) ) has narrated:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) prohibited any Muslim from relieving himself under a tree or palm that has borne fruit due to the presence of the angels assigned to them.
Imam (as) said: 'That is why trees and palms seem luster when they bear fruit, for the angels are present there.'".
records.arabicText: 64 - وَ إِنَّمَا نَهَى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أَنْ يَضْرِبَ أَحَدٌ مِنَ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ خَلاَءَهُ تَحْتَ شَجَرَةٍ أَوْ نَخْلَةٍ قَدْ أَثْمَرَتْ لِمَكَانِ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةِ اَلْمُوَكَّلِينَ بِهَا» قَالَ «وَ لِذَلِكَ يَكُونُ اَلشَّجَرُ وَ اَلنَّخْلُ أُنْساً إِذَا كَانَ فِيهِ حَمْلُهُ لِأَنَّ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةَ تَحْضُرُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.64 – (Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) ) has narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) prohibited any Muslim from relieving himself under a tree or palm that has borne fruit due to the presence of the angels assigned to them.
Imam (as) said: 'That is why trees and palms seem luster when they bear fruit, for the angels are present there.'".

records.chapter: Seeking the Place to Relieve Oneself and the Etiquettes of Entering and Exiting
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.65 - It was narrated that Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) would awaken from his sleep, perform ablution, and not perform istinja.
He expressed astonishment at a man whom he referred to, saying, "I have heard that when he passes gas, he performs istinja."
records.arabicText: 65 - وَ رُوِيَ: أَنَّ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَانَ يَسْتَيْقِظُ مِنْ نَوْمِهِ فَيَتَوَضَّأُ وَ لاَ يَسْتَنْجِي وَقَالَ كَالْمُتَعَجِّبِ مِنْ رَجُلٍ سَمَّاهُ «بَلَغَنِي أَنَّهُ إِذَا خَرَجَتْ مِنْهُ رِيحٌ اِسْتَنْجَى.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.65 - It was narrated that Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) would awaken from his sleep, perform ablution, and not perform istinja.
He expressed astonishment at a man whom he referred to, saying,
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I have heard that when he passes gas, he performs istinja."

records.chapter: Sections of Salat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.66 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The prayer consists of three parts: one-third is for purification, one-third is for bowing (ruku), and one-third is for prostration (sujud) "
records.arabicText: 66 - قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اَلصَّلاَةُ ثَلاَثَةُ أَثْلاَثٍ ثُلُثٌ طَهُورٌ وَ ثُلُثٌ رُكُوعٌ وَ ثُلُثٌ سُجُودٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.66 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The prayer consists of three parts: one-third is for purification, one-third is for bowing (ruku), and one-third is for prostration (sujud) "

records.chapter: The Times When Purification is an Obligation
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.67 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"When the time for prayer enters, purification (ablution) and the prayer become obligatory, and there is no prayer without purification."
records.arabicText: 67 - قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِذَا دَخَلَ اَلْوَقْتُ وَجَبَ اَلطَّهُورُ وَ اَلصَّلاَةُ وَ لاَ صَلاَةَ إِلاَّ بِطَهُورٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.67 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When the time for prayer enters, purification (ablution) and the prayer become obligatory, and there is no prayer without purification."

records.chapter: The Initiation of Salat, Its Prohibitions and Dissolution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.68 - The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), said:
"The initiation of prayer is purification (ablution), its prohibition is the takbir (saying 'Allahu Akbar'), and its permissibility is the Tasleem (saying 'Assalamu Alaikum')."
records.arabicText: 68 - قَالَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اِفْتِتَاحُ اَلصَّلاَةِ اَلْوُضُوءُ وَ تَحْرِيمُهَا اَلتَّكْبِيرُ وَ تَحْلِيلُهَا اَلتَّسْلِيمُ.

records.chapter: Obligations of Salat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: The obligations of Salat are seven: timing, purification, attention, facing the Qibla, bowing (Rukuh), prostration (Sujood) and supplication.
records.arabicText: فرائض الصلاة سبعة: الوقت، والطهور، والتوجه، والقبلة، والركوع، والسجود، والدعاء

records.chapter: The Amount of Water for Ablution and Ghusl
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.69 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
"For ghusl (ritual washing), a saa' of water is required, and for wudu (ablution), a mud of water is sufficient.
The saa' of The Prophet (sw) is five amdad (measures), and the mud weighs two hundred and eighty dirhams. One dirham is six danaq (small units), and one danaq weighs six grains.
The grain should be from medium-quality barley, not from the small or large types”.
records.arabicText: 69 - قَالَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «لِلْغُسْلِ صَاعٌ مِنْ مَاءٍ وَ لِلْوُضُوءِ مُدٌّ مِنْ مَاءٍ وَ صَاعُ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ خَمْسَةُ أَمْدَادٍ وَ اَلْمُدُّ وَزْنُ مِائَتَيْنِ وَ ثَمَانِينَ دِرْهَماً وَ اَلدِّرْهَمُ سِتَّةُ دَوَانِيقَ وَ اَلدَّانِقُ وَزْنُ سِتِّ حَبَّاتٍ وَ اَلْحَبَّةُ وَزْنُ حَبَّتَيْنِ مِنْ شَعِيرٍ مِنْ أَوْسَاطِ اَلْحَبِّ لاَ مِنْ صِغَارِهِ وَ لاَ مِنْ كِبَارِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.69 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"For ghusl (ritual washing), a saa' of water is required, and for wudu (ablution), a mud of water is sufficient.
The saa' of The Prophet (sw) is five amdad (measures), and the mud weighs two hundred and eighty dirhams. One dirham is six danaq (small units), and one danaq weighs six grains.
The grain should be from medium-quality barley, not from the small or large types”.

records.chapter: The Amount of Water for Ablution and Ghusl
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.70 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
"Ablution (wudu) is a mud (a specific measure), and ghusl (ritual washing) is a saa' (a larger measure). There will come people after me who will underestimate these amounts, and those will be contrary to my Sunnah.
The one who adheres to my Sunnah will be with me in the enclosure of holiness (Paradise)."
records.arabicText: 70 - وَقَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ: «اَلْوُضُوءُ مُدٌّ وَ اَلْغُسْلُ صَاعٌ وَ سَيَأْتِي أَقْوَامٌ بَعْدِي يَسْتَقِلُّونَ ذَلِكَ فَأُولَئِكَ عَلَى خِلاَفِ سُنَّتِي وَ اَلثَّابِتُ عَلَى سُنَّتِي مَعِي فِي حَظِيرَةِ اَلْقُدْسِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.70 - The Messenger of Allah (sw) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Ablution (wudu) is a mud (a specific measure), and ghusl (ritual washing) is a saa' (a larger measure). There will come people after me who will underestimate these amounts, and those will be contrary to my Sunnah.
The one who adheres to my Sunnah will be with me in the enclosure of holiness (Paradise)."

records.chapter: The Amount of Water for Ablution and Ghusl
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.71 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) was asked about a man who needed to perform wudu for prayer but could not find water, except for a quantity of water worth a hundred dirhams. Should he buy it to perform wudu or should he perform tayammum (dry ablution)?
Imam (as) said: ""Rather, he should buy it. I have experienced a similar situation, and I bought it and performed wudu and I would not mind having that money”.
records.arabicText: 71 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِحْتَاجَ إِلَى اَلْوُضُوءِ لِلصَّلاَةِ وَ لَمْ يَقْدِرْ عَلَى اَلْمَاءِ فَوَجَدَ مَاءً بِقَدْرِ مَا يَتَوَضَّأُ بِهِ بِمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ هَلْ يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ أَنْ يَشْتَرِيَهُ وَ يَتَوَضَّأَ بِهِ أَوْ يَتَيَمَّمَ فَقَالَ «بَلْ يَشْتَرِي قَدْ أَصَابَنِي مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ فَاشْتَرَيْتُ وَ تَوَضَّأْتُ وَ مَا يَسُوؤُنِي بِذَلِكَ مَالٌ كَثِيرٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.71 - Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as) was asked about a man who needed to perform wudu for prayer but could not find water, except for a quantity of water worth a hundred dirhams. Should he buy it to perform wudu or should he perform tayammum (dry ablution)?
Imam (as) said: "
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Rather, he should buy it. I have experienced a similar situation, and I bought it and performed wudu and I would not mind having that money”.

records.chapter: The Amount of Water for Ablution and Ghusl
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.72 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) and his wife performed ghusl using five amdad from one container."
Zurarah then asked how it was done.
Imam (as) said: "He started by immersing his hand in the water before hers and cleansed his private part. Then She did the same and cleansed her private part. After that, he poured water over himself, and She poured water over herself until they were finished. The amount that the Prophet used for ghusl was three amdad, while She used two muds. This was sufficient for both of them because they shared the same water.
However, for someone who performs ghusl alone, they must use a saa'."
records.arabicText:
72 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «اِغْتَسَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ هُوَ وَ زَوْجَتُهُ مِنْ خَمْسَةِ أَمْدَادٍ مِنْ إِنَاءٍ وَاحِدٍ» فَقَالَ لَهُ زُرَارَةُ كَيْفَ صَنَعَ فَقَالَ «بَدَأَ هُوَ فَضَرَبَ يَدَهُ فِي اَلْمَاءِ قَبْلَهَا فَأَنْقَى فَرْجَهُ ثُمَّ ضَرَبَتْ هِيَ فَأَنْقَتْ فَرْجَهَا ثُمَّ أَفَاضَ هُوَ وَ أَفَاضَتْ هِيَ عَلَى نَفْسِهَا حَتَّى فَرَغَا وَ كَانَ اَلَّذِي اِغْتَسَلَ بِهِ اَلنَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ ثَلاَثَةَ أَمْدَادٍ وَ اَلَّذِي اِغْتَسَلَتْ بِهِ مُدَّيْنِ وَ إِنَّمَا أَجْزَأَ عَنْهُمَا لِأَنَّهُمَا اِشْتَرَكَا فِيهِ جَمِيعاً وَ مَنِ اِنْفَرَدَ بِالْغُسْلِ وَحْدَهُ فَلاَ بُدَّ لَهُ مِنْ صَاعٍ.
.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.72 - Abu Ja'far Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"The Messenger of Allah (sw) and his wife performed ghusl using five amdad from one container."
Zurarah then asked how it was done.
Imam (as) said: "He started by immersing his hand in the water before hers and cleansed his private part. Then She did the same and cleansed her private part. After that, he poured water over himself, and She poured water over herself until they were finished. The amount that the Prophet used for ghusl was three amdad, while She used two muds. This was sufficient for both of them because they shared the same water.
However, for someone who performs ghusl alone, they must use a saa'."

records.chapter: The Amount of Water for Ablution and Ghusl
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.73 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"A man may worship Allah (swt) for forty years but does not truly obey Him in wudu (ablution) because he washes what Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, commanded him to wipe."
records.arabicText: 73 - وَقَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ اَلرَّجُلَ لَيَعْبُدُ اَللَّهَ أَرْبَعِينَ سَنَةً وَ مَا يُطِيعُهُ فِي اَلْوُضُوءِ لِأَنَّهُ يَغْسِلُ مَا أَمَرَ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ بِمَسْحِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.73 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A man may worship Allah (swt) for forty years but does not truly obey Him in wudu (ablution) because he washes what Allah (swt), Mighty and Majestic, commanded him to wipe."

records.chapter: The Description of the Ablution of the Messenger of Allah {saws}
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.74 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"Shall I not describe the ablution of the Messenger of Allah (sw) to you?"
They replied: "Yes."
He then called for a bowl containing some water and placed it in front of him.
He rolled up his sleeves, dipped his right hand into the water, and said:
"This is if the hand is pure."
He then scooped a full handful of water, placed it on his forehead, "In the name of Allah (swt)," allowing the water to flow over the edges of his beard.
Then he passed his hand over his face and the front of his temples once.
He dipped his left hand into the water, scooped a full handful, and placed it on his right elbow, running his hand along his forearm until the water flowed over the tips of his fingers.
He then scooped with his right hand again and placed it on his left elbow, running his hand along his forearm until the water flowed over the tips of his fingers.
He then wiped the front of his head and the tops of his feet with the leftover moisture from the water.
records.arabicText: 74 - قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ اَلْبَاقِرُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَ لاَ أَحْكِي لَكُمْ وُضُوءَ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ » فَقِيلَ لَهُ بَلَى فَدَعَا بِقَعْبٍ فِيهِ شَيْءٌ مِنْ مَاءٍ فَوَضَعَهُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ثُمَّ حَسَرَ عَنْ ذِرَاعَيْهِ ثُمَّ غَمَسَ فِيهِ كَفَّهُ اَلْيُمْنَى ثُمَّ قَالَ «هَذَا إِذَا كَانَتِ اَلْكَفُّ طَاهِرَةً» ثُمَّ غَرَفَ مِلْأَهَا مَاءً ثُمَّ وَضَعَهُ عَلَى جَبْهَتِهِ وَقَالَ « بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ » وَ سَيَّلَهُ عَلَى أَطْرَافِ لِحْيَتِهِ ثُمَّ أَمَرَّ يَدَهُ عَلَى وَجْهِهِ وَ ظَاهِرِ جَبِينَيْهِ مَرَّةً وَاحِدَةً ثُمَّ غَمَسَ يَدَهُ اَلْيُسْرَى فَغَرَفَ بِهَا مِلْأَهَا ثُمَّ وَضَعَهُ عَلَى مِرْفَقِهِ اَلْيُمْنَى فَأَمَرَّ كَفَّهُ عَلَى سَاعِدِهِ حَتَّى جَرَى اَلْمَاءُ عَلَى أَطْرَافِ أَصَابِعِهِ ثُمَّ غَرَفَ بِيَمِينِهِ مِلْأَهَا فَوَضَعَهُ عَلَى مِرْفَقِهِ اَلْأَيْسَرِ فَأَمَرَّ كَفَّهُ عَلَى سَاعِدِهِ حَتَّى جَرَى اَلْمَاءُ عَلَى أَطْرَافِ أَصَابِعِهِ وَ مَسَحَ عَلَى مُقَدَّمِ رَأْسِهِ وَ ظَهْرِ قَدَمَيْهِ بِبِلَّةِ بَقِيَّةِ مَائِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.74 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Shall I not describe the ablution of the Messenger of Allah (sw) to you?"
They replied: "Yes."
He then called for a bowl containing some water and placed it in front of him.
He rolled up his sleeves, dipped his right hand into the water, and said:
"This is if the hand is pure."
He then scooped a full handful of water, placed it on his forehead, "In the name of Allah (swt)," allowing the water to flow over the edges of his beard.
Then he passed his hand over his face and the front of his temples once.
He dipped his left hand into the water, scooped a full handful, and placed it on his right elbow, running his hand along his forearm until the water flowed over the tips of his fingers.
He then scooped with his right hand again and placed it on his left elbow, running his hand along his forearm until the water flowed over the tips of his fingers.
He then wiped the front of his head and the tops of his feet with the leftover moisture from the water.

records.chapter: The Description of the Ablution of the Messenger of Allah {saws}
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.75 - It was narrated that the Messenger of Allah (sw) performed wudu and then wiped over his sandals.
Al-Mughirah said to him: "Did you forget, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)?"
He (sw) replied: "No, rather it is you who has forgotten. This is how my Lord (azj) commanded me."
records.arabicText: 75 - وَ رُوِيَ: «أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ تَوَضَّأَ ثُمَّ مَسَحَ عَلَى نَعْلَيْهِ فَقَالَ لَهُ اَلْمُغِيرَةُ أَ نَسِيتَ يَا رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ قَالَ «بَلْ أَنْتَ نَسِيتَ هَكَذَا أَمَرَنِي رَبِّي».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.75 - It was narrated that the Messenger of Allah (sw) performed wudu and then wiped over his sandals.
Al-Mughirah said to him:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Did you forget, O’ Messenger of Allah (sw)?"
He (sw) replied: "No, rather it is you who has forgotten. This is how my Lord (azj) commanded me."
